new_classes.definition_en: A substance added to ethyl alcohol or spirits to render them unfit for human consumption, thereby constituting a denatured spirit under CN heading 2207 20.
A process in which a denaturing agent is added to ethyl alcohol or spirits of any alcoholic strength, making the product unfit for human consumption and classifiable under CN 2207 20.

new_classes.label_en: denatured ethyl alcohol and other spirits, of any strength (CN 2207 20)
new_classes.label_de: vergällter Ethylalkohol und Branntwein mit beliebigem Alkoholgehalt (KN 2207 20)
new_classes.definition_en: Ethyl alcohol and other spirits of any alcoholic strength that have been denatured by the addition of a denaturing agent, rendering them unfit for human consumption, classified under CN subheading 2207 20.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) the product is ethyl alcohol or other spirits (any alcoholic strength — no minimum or maximum % vol constraint, unlike CN 2207 10 which requires ≥ 80 % vol), (2) it has been denatured (isDenatured = true, has part a DenaturingAgent, produced by a DenaturingProcess), and (3) it is disjoint from the undenatured subheading (2207 10). A slight gap remains: the HS Explanatory Note fourth paragraph referenced in the legal text contains additional detail about which substances qualify as denaturing agents and the procedures required — these procedural/regulatory details cannot be fully captured in OWL DL axioms without introducing regulatory-process complexity beyond what is modellable. The "any strength" criterion (no alcohol-by-volume floor) is implicitly captured by the absence of an alcoholicStrength restriction, though a complement axiom vis-à-vis the ≥ 80 % vol class would be redundant and potentially inconsistent given that denatured alcohol can also be ≥ 80 % vol.
